title: Internal Data Format
text: Internal Data Format The internal format is a standard format based on the data type of the data concerned, usually combined with the packing code definition. No additional external formatting (such as a display format) is applied. Uniface handles all data in components in the internal format. The internal formats for handling Date, Time, Datetime, and Boolean data types are shown in the table: Table 1. Internal Formats for Handling Date, Time, Datetime, and Boolean Data Types Type Internal Format Example Date ccyymmdd 19940325 Time hhnnsstt 23595900 Datetime ccyymmddhhnnsstt 1994032523595900 Boolean F or T F For example, consider the field MYDATE with data type D, packing code D2, and display format DIS(DD-MM-YY). The date on the screen appears in the form 09-03-92. However, after the statement, the variable $1 contains 19920903, a date in the format ccyymmdd (the internal format for data type D) and not DD-MM-YY. : $1 = MYDATE Copy Uniface converts to external format—that is, the format associated with the packing code and display format—when the data is displayed or when an implicit type conversion occurs. Implicit type conversion can occur when you use, for example, relational operators between nonmatching types. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
